meta.status: POPULATED — live web research completed. DO NOT OVERWRITE — populated by research team.
sources: Belfast City Council — Belfast Castle wedding packages April 2025–March 2026
Belfast City Council — Malone House wedding packages and room hire rates 2025–27

marketOverview.trendNote: 2022 figure elevated by post-COVID pent-up demand. Underlying run-rate approximately 7,500–8,000/yr.
marketOverview.averageTotalSpendNI: £20,000–£25,000 per wedding (NI aligns with UK average; Republic of Ireland average is €33,000)
marketOverview.heritageSegmentEstimate: Approximately 15–20% of Belfast weddings book a heritage or country house venue
marketOverview.totalBelfastVenueMarketEstimate: ~4,500 greater Belfast weddings × ~£6,500 avg venue spend = ~£29m/year
trends: Post-COVID micro-weddings (under 40 guests) remain popular — intimate heritage venues well-positioned
Heritage and country house aesthetic dominates NI wedding social media — drives strong self-referral enquiry to authentic venues
Outdoor and garden ceremony demand has grown steadily since 2021
Growing segment of couples actively choosing venues that give back to community — aligns with Fernhill's model

venues.name: Belfast Castle
venues.includes: Full ground floor — Donegall Room (ceremony/arrival/evening), Chichester Room (reception), Deerpark Room, bridal suite, gardens, PA system, red carpet, table plan
venues.catering: In-house hospitality and catering (CRG Catering) — BCC published provider
venues.fernhill_comparison: Direct heritage comparator. Serves north/central Belfast. BCC-managed pricing benchmark; high weekend demand reported anecdotally — occupancy to be confirmed via officer/management enquiry. BCC management limits commercial agility.

venues.name: Malone House
venues.includes: Hilsborowe Room (ceremony/arrival 2hrs), Harberton Room (reception 3pm–1am), Malone Room bar (6pm–1am), wedding changing room, gardens, red carpet, table plan, PA
venues.fernhill_comparison: Closest format comparator (Georgian BCC heritage house). Serves south Belfast (BT9) — different geography from Fernhill (Glencairn, west/northwest BT13). Published pricing and package structure are the primary benchmark; peak-weekend demand supports citywide heritage wedding appetite.

venues.name: The Merchant Hotel
venues.notes: Premium city centre tier. No gardens. Different segment — not a direct competitor to Fernhill.

venues.name: Larchfield Estate
venues.location: Lisburn, Co. Antrim BT27 6XP (~20 min from south Belfast)
venues.notes: Popular and frequently fully booked at weekends — strong demand signal for heritage estate format beyond BCC venues.
venues.fernhill_comparison: Important benchmark. Same heritage estate format. Weekend availability often sold out months ahead — confirms strong unmet demand.

venues.name: Killeavy Castle Estate
venues.location: Meigh, Co. Armagh BT35 8SW (~1hr from Belfast)
venues.notes: Destination wedding market. Rural Co. Armagh. Different proposition to Fernhill's in-city offer.

geographicGapAnalysis.finding: West and northwest Belfast (Glencairn, Ballygomartin, Shankill interface, Forthriver corridor) has no BCC-managed heritage wedding house equivalent to Malone House or Belfast Castle. Malone House serves south Belfast; Belfast Castle serves north Belfast. Fernhill would be the first heritage-house wedding offer anchored in the Glencairn parkland setting.
geographicGapAnalysis.demandModel_note: Derived metric — not a verified statistic. Requires demand study before funding submission.

demandModel_inputs.citywideHeritageVenues_note: Malone House and Belfast Castle absorb some citywide demand but are not west-Belfast-local

geographicGapAnalysis.unmet_demand_estimate_derived: Approximately 40–55 couples per year in west/northwest Belfast may seek a local heritage-house format if one existed — subject to formal demand survey.
geographicGapAnalysis.competitionIntensity: LOW–MEDIUM for intimate (60–120 guest) heritage house segment in west/northwest Belfast; MEDIUM citywide when private estates included

fernhillPositioning.proposedTier: Mid-premium heritage — accessible elegance. Below Merchant Hotel/Larchfield premium; above standard hotel ballroom. Comparable to Malone House but independently governed with greater commercial flexibility.
keyDifferentiators: First heritage-house wedding venue anchored in west/northwest Belfast (Glencairn Park)
Community-owned — couples actively choose Fernhill to support something meaningful
Walled gardens for outdoor ceremonies and photography
Intimate scale — every wedding is the only event of the day
Victorian heritage narrative — genuine building story, not a purpose-built events space
Restoration story adds depth and PR value couples want to share on social media
